We've been in Singapore for nearly eight years now. We live in Little India. But this is the first time. We are attending the Pongal festival. My daughter is half Indian, so her dad is Indian, so it's really nice to have the music and offerings they have here. 

We came here, they gave us Pongal, and then we had it. It was really nice. 

We came today because work is over and we wanted to celebrate Pongal. Even though it's raining, we came to celebrate Indian Pongal. <Speaker 1>: Every festivity we have here in Singapore is a celebration of our people, of our nation. So, (uh), an event like Pongal celebration, (uh), is a representation of how we as a Singapore community come together regardless of race, language, or religion, not only to celebrate the festivity, but also to see the values behind the festivity. Like in this case, I'm very happy to see the value of gratitude, right, where we not only celebrate and we, we appreciate. <Speaker 1>: what we have, but also how we give back, how we share the joy or happiness, prosperity that we have, and I'm happy to see the younger generation coming forward this year where they come, contribute, they engage the seniors and they want to be part of this and they feel happy doing it. So we are on the right track and we should continue this journey.

திருப்பகல் முதல் இடைவிடாத மழை பெய்த போதிலும், 
சிறிய இந்தியா வர்த்தகர்கள் மறுமொழிமை சங்கம் ஏற்பாடு செய்திருந்த பொங்கல் ஒளிஉத்து விழாவை காண மக்கள் பொலி திறந்த வெளியில் அமைக்கப்பட்ட கூடாரத்தில் திரண்டனர்.
